Final Year Design Project · NED UEIT · 2025–2026

VR-Based Physical Rehabilitation System

PhysioGuide combines Oculus Quest 2, real-time AI pose tracking and a virtual therapy gym to bring clinical-grade physiotherapy into any home.

Real-Time Tracking
The Challenge & Our Answer

Transforming Rehab
from Clinic to Home

Millions of patients face barriers to consistent physiotherapy — PhysioGuide removes every single one.

⚠️ The Problem

Therapist unavailability, travel barriers, and patients unable to self-monitor correct exercise form at home — leading to poor rehabilitation outcomes and slow recovery.

✦ Our Solution

An immersive VR gym with AI pose tracking that guides, corrects, and logs patient exercises — with remote therapist monitoring via a live dashboard for asynchronous review.

How It Works

System Pipeline

Five seamlessly connected stages from headset to therapist dashboard.

01

VR Entry

Patient wears the Oculus Quest 2 and enters the virtual therapy gym — a realistic 3D space with anatomical trainer avatars that demonstrate each exercise with correct form.

02

Pose Capture

A webcam or depth sensor streams live video. MediaPipe extracts 33 skeletal joint keypoints per frame at 30 FPS, capturing precise body position and limb angles in real time.

03

AI Analysis

Joint angles and posture are compared against clinically validated ideal therapy models. The system measures deviations within ±5–10° tolerance per exercise — including Scaption (85–95°), Chin Tuck, Elbow Flexion, and Wrist Extension.

04

Live Feedback

Color-coded cues (green = correct, red = incorrect), on-screen text prompts, and audio guidance correct movement instantly. ZeroMQ bridges feedback from the PC to the headset in under 50ms.

05

Therapist Dashboard

Session logs, rep counts, accuracy scores, and progress charts are sent to a remote therapist interface for asynchronous review — clinically validated by Dr. Wasi at NeuroSpinal & Cancer Care Institute.

Technical Architecture

4-Layer System Design

A modular, dual-stream architecture built for low latency and high reliability.

🧥

VR Layer

Unity3D virtual gym with data-driven anatomical avatars on Oculus Quest 2. Immersive environment with exercise demonstrations and spatial audio feedback.

👁️

Vision Pipeline

MediaPipe pose estimation at 30 FPS on a standard webcam. Extracts 33 body landmarks per frame with 100ms end-to-end latency for the perception layer.

🧠

AI Analysis Engine

Biomechanical comparison of live joint angles against ideal therapy models. Covers 35+ upper-body exercises with clinically validated angle thresholds.

⚡

Communication Bus

ZeroMQ message broker bridges the Python analysis server and Unity3D runtime at 50ms latency — enabling stable, real-time feedback delivery to the headset.

📊

Therapist Interface

React + Django web portal for remote therapists. Session replays, rep-by-rep accuracy charts, trend analytics, and patient progress over time.

☁️

Backend & Data

FastAPI/Django REST backend with PostgreSQL. Session data, posture logs, and patient metrics stored securely for asynchronous therapist review and longitudinal tracking.
